<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570464 space=MMA version=5 -->
## PAGE 00518: Activity duration simulation

- page_id: `249570464`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570464/Activity+duration+simulation
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-16T10:03:08.794+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Activity simulation
- labels: ['activity-duration']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can simulate the time required to implement an Activity as specified by the duration constraints. It calculates the total Activity duration by adding the simulation time of all visited Actions, and aids in accurately identifying which activities take the longest time to complete. You can see full details about the Activity duration for simulation, including the Activity names and the time between the start and finish of an Activity, in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation console' space=''] by setting the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title="Console log's filter options" space=''] to **Info**. An Activity can contain many Actions. You can specify a duration constraint on any Action in the Activity. The minimum and maximum duration of an Action can be provided through the **min** and **max** of the duration constraint. If you simulate the Activity with a specific duration mode, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will compute the total time spent when simulating the Activity for you.

When a Behavior's duration is empty or the time is unspecified, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will use the duration of called Actions as the duration of the Behavior.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If a Behavior has a property or a structural feature value named **duration**, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will automatically assign the duration value to that particular feature value at runtime. The duration value property must be defined in the Activity, e.g., Change Color as shown in the following figure, or the parent of the duration value property. You can have only one duration value property in the parent Activity to avoid creating multiple duration value properties.

###### The duration value property is automatically assigned during simulation runtime.

##### Supported time units

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports extensive Time Units (suffixes) in duration constraints, as well as Time Units of [Simulation Configuration](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/SimulationConfig+stereotype), accept time Event, and others. The following list summarizes these suffixes

- ns, nsec, nanosecond, nanoseconds
- µs, microsec, microsecond, microseconds
- ms, millisec, millisecond, milliseconds (by default)
- s, sec, second, seconds
- m, min, minute, minutes (All UI, e.g. Charts, CSVExport, etc., will be shown as "min" instead of "m" for meters.)
- h, hr, hrs, hour, hours
- d, day, days
- wk, week, weeks
- mo, month, months
- y, a, yr, year, years

You can parse the Time Unit specified as a suffix after a number or at the end of an expression string. However, floating numbers in duration constraints are not supported. Floating numbers are rounded to integers instead of automatically converted to smaller units. If you do not specify a suffix, the millisecond will be used by default. The examples are as follows

- 100m or 100min is considered 100 minutes.
- xs is considered the value of the property x in seconds.
- 200 is considered 200 milliseconds. NoteIf the Time Unit is less than 1 nanosecond, e.g. 0.5 nanosecond or 0.0005 microsecond, a warning message is displayed in the **Console** pane stating that the default value of 1 nanosecond is applied.Nanosecond and microsecond are supported only for the simulation clock and model based clock, but they are not supported for the built-in PC clock because the returned time of the PC clock using System.currentTimeMillis() is in millisecond. See also in [Simulation time and simulation clock](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Simulation+time+and+simulation+clock).

##### Duration simulation modes

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports four duration simulation modes.

| Duration Simulation Mode | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Min | When you select min as the duration simulation mode, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will increase the time spent on an Activity by the min duration specified on the duration constraint when an element with an applied duration constraint is activated. |
| Max | If you select max as the duration simulation mode, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will increase the time spent on an Activity by the max duration specified on the duration constraint when an element with an applied duration constraint is activated. |
| Average | When Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates your model with the average duration simulation mode, it will use the average value between the max and min duration of the duration constraint as the duration of time spent on simulating an element with an applied duration constraint. |
| Random | The random mode allows Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to obtain the duration of time spent on simulating an element with an applied duration constraint from a uniformly distributed random number between the min and max duration of the duration constraint. |

If you specify a duration simulation mode, the time spent on an Activity simulation will be calculated from the duration constraint applied to the elements of the Activity. The duration of time spent on simulating elements with no duration constraints will be zero. If you simulate a model without specifying any duration simulation mode, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will ignore those duration constraints and obtain the time spent on simulating the Actions and activities from the simulation clock.

The Duration Simulation modes (min, max, average, and random) are available in the model's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']. You can specify the duration constraints of the Activity element by entering the value in the element's **Specification** window.

##### Running an Activity simulation

The [DurationConstraint.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/20851829/DurationConstraint.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1505277500565&api=v2) sample is used as an example to explain how the Activity duration simulation works.

To specify the duration simulation mode and the duration constraints of an Activity, and run the Activity simulation

1. Either double-click or right-click a «[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SimulationConfig stereotype' space='']» (e.g., *winter*) on the diagram pane and select Specification to open the Specification window.
2. From the Duration Simulation Mode property, select a duration type (e.g., min). The selected duration mode will appear in the «SimulationConfig». [ATTACHMENT filename='Spec_durationAnalysis.png']
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Close_button.png'] .
4. Open the rainy day scenario on the diagram pane and open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT'] of each element to specify the duration constraint for the selected duration simulation mode. In this example, double-click 'dig the snow' and specify the duration constraint value. [ATTACHMENT filename='DurationConstraint_analysis.png']
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Close_button.png'] .
6. Right-click the «SimulationConfig» or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the toolbar to run the simulation. View the simulation results of the Activity duration in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Console pane' space='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Right-clicking the «SimulationConfig» on the diagram pane to select the Simulation menu to run the simulation of the «SimulationConfig».**[IMAGE alt='' src='']** The Run toolbar button triggers the command to run the simulation of the «SimulationConfig». [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Console tab in the Simulation window contains the simulation of Activity duration of the DurationConstraint.mdzip sample from start to finish.

If you simulate a call Behavior Action having a duration constraint with a duration simulation mode and the time spent on the called Behavior is beyond the range of the duration constraint specified on the call Behavior Action, the call Behavior Action will be considered as a broken constraint element. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will then pause the model simulation at the call Behavior Action.

##### Duration constraints on Activities

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit also supports simulation when you define a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Duration constraint' space=''] for an Activity itself apart from a Call Behavior Action. The duration constraint on the Activity is interpreted the same as the duration constraint on the Call Behavior Action. The duration constraint can be shown in the Timeline chart or on the **Console** pane. In the following figure, the duration constraint is shown on the **Console** pane.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']A duration constraint is applied on the Activity as shown on the Console pane.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the duration constraints are defined on both Activity and Call Behavior Action, the constraints of the Call Behavior Action will be used, and the constraints of the Activity will be ignored.

##### Glossary

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | The duration simulation mode specifies the duration to run the simulation of the elements with applied duration constraints. |
|  | The duration constraint is an interval constraint that refers to a duration interval. Use the duration interval to determine whether the constraint is satisfied. |
|  | The call Behavior Action is a call Action that invokes a Behavior directly rather than invoking an operation that invokes the Behavior. |

**Related pages**

- Creating a model for Activity simulation
- Duration analysis
- Duration constraint
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MT']

**Sample model**

- [ATTACHMENT filename='DurationConstraint.mdzip']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can simulate the time required to implement an Activity as specified by the duration constraints. It calculates the total Activity duration by adding the simulation time of all visited Actions, and aids in accurately identifying which activities take the longest time to complete. You can see full details about the Activity duration for simulation, including the Activity names and the time between the start and finish of an Activity, in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Simulation console" /></ac:link> by setting the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Console log's filter options" /></ac:link> to <strong>Info</strong>. An Activity can contain many Actions. You can specify a duration constraint on any Action in the Activity. The minimum and maximum duration of an Action can be provided through the <strong>min</strong> and <strong>max</strong> of the duration constraint. If you simulate the Activity with a specific duration mode, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will compute the total time spent when simulating the Activity for you.</span></p><p><span>When a Behavior's duration is empty or the time is unspecified, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will use the duration of called Actions as the duration of the Behavior.</span></p><p><span> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d85f8dd-7ae9-4b0b-abe3-1be4f2c23c6b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If a Behavior has a property or a structural feature value named <strong>duration</strong>, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will automatically assign the duration value to that particular feature value at runtime. The duration value property must be defined in the Activity, e.g., Change Color as shown in the following figure, or the parent of the duration value property. You can have only one duration value property in the parent Activity to avoid creating multiple duration value properties.</span></p><p><span> </span></p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Duration Property Value.PNG" /></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">The duration value property is automatically assigned during simulation runtime.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Supported time units</h3><p class="Textbody">Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports extensive Time Units (suffixes) in duration constraints, as well as Time Units of <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/SimulationConfig+stereotype">Simulation Configuration</a>, accept time Event, and others. The following list summarizes these suffixes</p><ul><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="4d5e9ff2-896b-429d-8377-8720ceeedc7d">ns, nsec, nanosecond, nanoseconds</li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="0a547f74-c3f0-4388-97a6-e6fbfe3595ef"><p class="auto-cursor-target">µs, microsec, microsecond, microseconds</p></li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="92392f70-9174-4e83-8639-b130df945165">ms, millisec, millisecond, milliseconds (by default)</li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="5966477d-5940-4edb-bd76-71a2125dae22">s, sec, second, seconds</li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="5683c27f-ed25-44c7-8d94-b415adb9e6cc">m, min, minute, minutes (All UI, e.g. Charts, CSVExport, etc., will be shown as &quot;min&quot; instead of &quot;m&quot; for meters.)</li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="b879c2a9-d3a8-427a-8586-f842247f303a">h, hr, hrs, hour, hours</li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="9299f4c9-d0c5-48b0-9dde-48998e7cd8aa">d, day, days</li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="366f6d6f-0e65-4b72-9763-69ae431978a2">wk, week, weeks</li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="111129e2-ff0b-4e2e-ae92-0972f363ac15">mo, month, months</li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="1db87d33-ace1-4e96-9f5f-d6975becacc3">y, a, yr, year, years</li></ul><p class="Textbody">You can parse the Time Unit specified as a suffix after a number or at the end of an expression string. However, floating numbers in duration constraints are not supported. Floating numbers are rounded to integers instead of automatically converted to smaller units. If you do not specify a suffix, the millisecond will be used by default. The examples are as follows</p><ul><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="1a90c213-cc28-4e97-a0bb-cdcdc54ec137">100m or 100min is considered 100 minutes.</li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="066810df-1089-4d9b-9ba2-ea62396b038a">xs is considered the value of the property x in seconds.</li><li class="Textbody" data-uuid="ae072fc4-49f9-47a2-860e-9fc956881789"><p class="auto-cursor-target">200 is considered 200 milliseconds.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a44e05c1-2bf6-4d33-bece-5cd22e1603dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the Time Unit is less than 1 nanosecond, e.g. 0.5 nanosecond or 0.0005 microsecond, a warning message is displayed in the <strong>Console</strong> pane stating that the default value of 1 nanosecond is applied.</li><li>Nanosecond and microsecond are supported only for the simulation clock and model based clock, but they are not supported for the built-in PC clock because the returned time of the PC clock using System.currentTimeMillis() is in millisecond. See also in <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Simulation+time+and+simulation+clock">Simulation time and simulation clock</a>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h3>Duration simulation modes</h3><p><span>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports four duration simulation modes. </span></p><p><br /></p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 18.0589%;" /><col class="" style="width: 81.9411%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Duration Simulation Mode</span></th><th><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Description</span></th></tr><tr class=""><td><span><strong>Min</strong> </span></td><td><span>When you select min as the duration simulation mode, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will increase the time spent on an Activity by the min duration specified on the duration constraint when an element with an applied duration constraint is activated.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><span><strong>Max</strong></span></td><td><span>If you select max as the duration simulation mode, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will increase the time spent on an Activity by the max duration specified on the duration constraint when an element with an applied duration constraint is activated. </span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><span><strong>Average</strong> </span></td><td><span>When Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit simulates your model with the average duration simulation mode, it will use the average value between the max and min duration of the duration constraint as the duration of time spent on simulating an element with an applied duration constraint. </span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><span><strong>Random</strong> </span></td><td><span>The random mode allows Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to obtain the duration of time spent on simulating an element with an applied duration constraint from a uniformly distributed random number between the min and max duration of the duration constraint. </span></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><span>If you specify a duration simulation mode, the time spent on an Activity simulation will be calculated from the duration constraint applied to the elements of the Activity. The duration of time spent on simulating elements with no duration constraints will be zero. If you simulate a model without specifying any duration simulation mode, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will ignore those duration constraints and obtain the time spent on simulating the Actions and activities from the simulation clock.</span></p><p><span>The Duration Simulation modes (min, max, average, and random) are available in the model's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. You can specify the duration constraints of the Activity element by entering the value in the element's <strong>Specification</strong> window.<br /></span></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Running an Activity simulation</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>The <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/20851829/DurationConstraint.mdzip?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1505277500565&amp;api=v2">DurationConstraint.mdzip</a> sample is used as an example to explain how the Activity duration simulation works.</span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To specify the duration simulation mode and the duration constraints of an Activity, and run the Activity simulation</span> </p><hr /><p><br /></p><ol><li data-uuid="b8ffe28e-361e-4851-8fd5-2c702b47f501"><p>Either double-click or right-click a «<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SimulationConfig stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SimulationConfig]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>»  (e.g., <em>winter</em>) on the diagram pane and select Specification to open the Specification window.</p></li><li data-uuid="77940209-0664-4175-a527-88b2ea8b52da">From the <strong>Duration Simulation Mode</strong> property, select a duration type (e.g., min). The selected duration mode will appear in the «SimulationConfig».<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Spec_durationAnalysis.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="f6ee413a-6928-416e-8c57-02eb1084f79d">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Close_button.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li data-uuid="1e657ece-6946-4b80-9b67-439747af9acf">Open the rainy day scenario on the diagram pane and open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of each element to specify the duration constraint for the selected duration simulation mode. In this example, double-click 'dig the snow' and specify the duration constraint value.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="DurationConstraint_analysis.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="1c870176-6298-4f77-ab49-dbeabbda4d98">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Close_button.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li data-uuid="12d2729f-4231-4edf-9013-88c0643def0e"><p>Right-click the «SimulationConfig» or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Run_button.png" /></ac:image> on the toolbar to run the simulation. View the simulation results of the Activity duration in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Console pane" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Simulation window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h6><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="RunSimulation.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Right-clicking the «SimulationConfig» on the diagram pane to select the Simulation menu to run the simulation of the «SimulationConfig».<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);"><strong><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="RunSimulation_button.png" /></ac:image></strong></span></h6><h6>The Run toolbar button triggers the command to run the simulation of the «SimulationConfig».</h6><h6><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="DurationConstraint_simulation.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6>The Console tab in the Simulation window contains the simulation of Activity duration of the DurationConstraint.mdzip sample from start to finish.</h6></li></ol><p>If you simulate a call Behavior Action having a duration constraint with a duration simulation mode and the time spent on the called Behavior is beyond the range of the duration constraint specified on the call Behavior Action, the call Behavior Action will be considered as a broken constraint element. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will then pause the model simulation at the call Behavior Action.</p><h3>Duration constraints on Activities</h3><p>Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit also supports simulation when you define a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Duration constraint" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[duration constraint]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for an Activity itself apart from a Call Behavior Action. The duration constraint on the Activity is interpreted the same as the duration constraint on the Call Behavior Action. The duration constraint can be shown in the Timeline chart or on the <strong>Console</strong> pane. In the following figure, the duration constraint is shown on the <strong>Console</strong> pane.</p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Duration Constraints on Activities.png" /></ac:image>A duration constraint is applied on the Activity as shown on the Console pane.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f953627d-095d-4770-9d74-1822db7fcfa4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the duration constraints are defined on both Activity and Call Behavior Action, the constraints of the Call Behavior Action will be used, and the constraints of the Activity will be ignored.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Glossary</span></h3><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Concept</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity" /></ac:link></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>The duration simulation mode specifies the duration to run the simulation of the elements with applied duration constraints.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Duration constraint" /></ac:link></td><td><span>The duration constraint is an interval constraint that refers to a duration interval. Use the duration interval to determine whether the constraint is satisfied.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity Partition execution and allocated Behavior" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Call Behavior Action]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td><td><span>The call Behavior Action is a call Action that invokes a Behavior directly rather than invoking an operation that invokes the Behavior.</span></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="f2838325-386c-4496-b304-b64ed8e74b92"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Creating+a+model+for+Activity+simulation">Creating a model for Activity simulation</a></li><li data-uuid="6bfa1554-8d97-42d3-ae32-e47f6ce2f402"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Duration+analysis">Duration analysis</a></li><li data-uuid="e00f6cda-d70d-462e-aee8-0aac80e569fa"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Duration+constraint">Duration constraint</a></li><li data-uuid="b20f473c-8ecb-4238-9930-5e0982a71285"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="DurationConstraint.mdzip" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570494 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00519: Activity Partition execution and allocated Behavior

- page_id: `249570494`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570494/Activity+Partition+execution+and+allocated+Behavior
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:12.793+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Activity simulation
- labels: ['activity-partition-execution']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Activity Partition is an element in an Activity diagram. It sets the boundary of simulation of an execution. If a Partition represents a property or a Classifier, all Actions and Activity calls inside the Partition will be run using the context of the property or the Classifier, which is represented on the Partition.

If the Activity Partition represents a part/property, the object playing the Partition is the object playing a part/ property that is represented by the Partition. If the Partition represents an InstanceSpecification, the object which is initialized from such instance specification shall be the object playing the Partition (if there is no object initialized from such instance specification yet, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit shall initialized when the Behavior execution of the Activity is initialized.

When multiple objects are playing an Activity Partition, only the first object that is not busy shall be the one that performs the Action in the Partition and it shall be the context of the Behavior execution invoked by the Action. This behavior can be changed by setting the **Allow Concurrent Allocated Activities** project option or Simulation Configuration property to *true*. When this property is set to *true*, allocated activities are executed simultaneously.

The following figure shows an example of the **(Activity)** **system** diagram on which a call Behavior Action is placed within an Activity Partition. The context of the Activity Partition is the runtime objects of workers. When the **system** model was run, the call Behavior Action was executed in the context of the Activity Partition: **workers**. But since there were more than one worker, the simulation searched for a worker that was not busy to perform the Behavior. If all the workers were busy, the simulation would wait until one of them was available. If the call Behavior Action were outside the Activity Partition, the context of the Behavior execution would be the **system** itself. The same also goes for an opaque Action. If it is in the Activity Partition, the simulation will run the opaque Action using the context of the property or the Classifier represented on the Partition.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Activity Partition simulation.

When a call Behavior Action is in an Activity Partition, the following conditions apply

- If the Behavior of the Action belongs to a Classifier of the object playing the Partition (or super type of such Classifier), the context of the Behavior execution shall be the object playing the Partition.
- If the Behavior does not belong to the Classifier of the object playing the Partition, but it belongs to the Classifier of the object that is the context of the Activity containing the Action (or super type of such Classifier), the Behavior execution shall be invoked in the context object of the Activity containing the Action.
- For other cases that cannot be categorized to either (i) or (ii), for example, the Behavior is an OpaqueBehavior or an OpaqueFunction, the context of the Behavior execution shall be the object playing the Partition.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Activity Partition is an element in an Activity diagram. It sets the boundary of simulation of an execution. If a Partition represents a property or a Classifier, all Actions and Activity calls inside the Partition will be run using the context of the property or the Classifier, which is represented on the Partition. </p><p>If the Activity Partition represents a part/property, the object playing the Partition is the object playing a part/ property that is represented by the Partition. If the Partition represents an InstanceSpecification, the object which is initialized from such instance specification shall be the object playing the Partition (if there is no object initialized from such instance specification yet, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit shall initialized when the Behavior execution of the Activity is initialized. </p><p>When multiple objects are playing an Activity Partition, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="46622b15-c022-4b26-874d-e1241fe4792a">only the first object that is not busy shall be the one that performs</ac:inline-comment-marker> the Action in the Partition and it shall be the context of the Behavior execution invoked by the Action. This behavior can be changed by setting the <strong>Allow Concurrent Allocated Activities</strong> project option or Simulation Configuration property to <em>true</em>. When this property is set to <em>true</em>, allocated activities are executed simultaneously.</p><p>The following figure shows an example of the <strong>(Activity)</strong> <strong>system</strong> diagram on which a call Behavior Action is placed within an Activity Partition. The context of the Activity Partition is the runtime objects of workers. When the <strong>system</strong> model was run, the call Behavior Action was executed in the context of the Activity Partition: <strong>workers</strong>. But since there were more than one worker, the simulation searched for a worker that was not busy to perform the Behavior. If all the workers were busy, the simulation would wait until one of them was available. If the call Behavior Action were outside the Activity Partition, the context of the Behavior execution would be the <strong>system</strong> itself. The same also goes for an opaque Action. If it is in the Activity Partition, the simulation will run the opaque Action using the context of the property or the Classifier represented on the Partition. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Activity Partition Simulation" ac:alt="Activity Partition Simulation"><ri:attachment ri:filename="SwimlaneExec_new.png" /></ac:image><br />Activity Partition simulation.</h6><p>When a call Behavior Action is in an Activity Partition, the following conditions apply</p><ul><li>If the Behavior of the Action belongs to a Classifier of the object playing the Partition (or super type of such Classifier), the context of the Behavior execution shall be the object playing the Partition.</li><li>If the Behavior does not belong to the Classifier of the object playing the Partition, but it belongs to the Classifier of the object that is the context of the Activity containing the Action (or super type of such Classifier), the Behavior execution shall be invoked in the context object of the Activity containing the Action.</li><li>For other cases that cannot be categorized to either (i) or (ii), for example, the Behavior is an OpaqueBehavior or an OpaqueFunction, the context of the Behavior execution shall be the object playing the Partition. </li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570399 space=MMA version=5 -->
## PAGE 00520: Activity simulation

- page_id: `249570399`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570399/Activity+simulation
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:07:56.871+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide
- labels: ['activity-simulation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

128423460

128423462

128423461

Activity simulations essentially require the Activity simulation engine in Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to perform all Activity-based simulations. The details are outlined in the following topics:

- Activity simulation engine Running Activity Simulation in Activity diagrams or Activity elements.
- Creating a model for Activity simulation Simulating Classifiers defined by Activities.
- Executing Activities Suspending the simulation with breakpoints.
- Activity duration simulation Calculating and analyzing Activity duration and specifying duration constraints.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Duration analysis' space='']Duration analysis Directing target objects to the callOperationAction.
- Running a Call Action simulation without a target pin Simulating a CallOperationAction that does not have a target pin to select a runtime object as a target.
- Activity Partition execution and allocated Behavior Using Activity Partition as an element in the Activity diagram to set the boundary of simulation of an execution.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Execution of incomplete or dummy models' space='']Execution of incomplete/dummy models Executing incomplete and dummy models with Behaviors of Actions when input or output pins or any properties of Actions are not specified or present.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using utility functions of Simulation' space='']Using utility functions of Simulation Facilitating common tasks through Opaque Behaviors under SimulationProfile::library::utils .

/></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Running+a+Call+Action+simulation+without+a+target+pin">Running a Call Action simulation without a target pin<br /></a>Simulating a CallOperationAction that does not have a target pin to select a runtime object as a target.<br /><br /></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Activity+Partition+execution+and+allocated+Behavior">Activity Partition execution and allocated Behavior<br /></a>Using Activity Partition as an element in incomplete/dummy models<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Executing incomplete and dummy models with Behaviors of Actions when input or output pins or any properties of Actions are not specified or common tasks through Opaque Behaviors under <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570400 space=MMA version=5 -->
## PAGE 00521: Activity simulation engine

- page_id: `249570400`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570400/Activity+simulation+engine
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-12T15:11:54.268+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Activity simulation
- labels: ['activity-simulation-engine']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

129494513

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

129494516

INLINE

1264288996

INLINE

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

129494514

INLINE

Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit provides an Activity simulation engine that allows you to run an Activity Simulation on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='MT'] or Activity Elements. Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit also includes the implementation of OMG Semantics of a Foundational Subset for Executable UML Models (fUML), an executable subset of standard UML, that can be used to define the structural and Behavioral semantics of systems. fUML defines a basic virtual machine for the Unified Modeling Language and supports specific abstractions enabling compliant models to be transformed into various executable forms for verification, integration, and deployment.

Various UML Activity diagram concepts are supported, including Object and Control Flows, Behavior and Operation Calls, sending Signals via Connectors with or without Ports in Internal Structure, accepting Signals and Time Events, Pins, Parameters, Decisions, Structured Activity Nodes, and many more.

The Activity simulation engine features include the following

- fUML 1.3 specification support.
- Any Action languages in opaqueBehaviors, opaqueExpressions, Decisions, Guards, and Constraints (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Integration with MATLAB' space=''] for more details).
- CallBehaviorAction with nested diagrams simulation and animation.
- SendSignalAction to send a Signal to a global Event queue to be used by the system level but not by the subsystem, e.g., in State Machine. NoteSendSignalAction has the **Target** input Pin for specifying the signal receiver. If this Pin is omitted, the signal instance will be sent to the context itself.The **On Port** property on SendSignalAction is used for sending signals via the specified port.
- CallOperationAction through a Port.
- Sending Signals through a Port.
- Support for Decision Nodes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Probability' space=''] over all outgoing edges.
- Support for Decision Nodes with a Decision input that provides input to Guard specifications on outgoing edges from each Decision Node.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The supported Decision input for Decision nodes.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- You can simulate only Activities that are owned by a Package or a Class. As a workaround, the CallBehavior Actions, owned by the Call Behaviors in a Package, will be used for the entry/do/exit Behaviors in States.

- The Guards on an ObjectFlow are not Boolean expressions in fUML. They should contain a value that matches the runtime value that flows on the ObjectFlow during simulation.

To change to a regular UML (Boolean expression)

1. On the main menu, click Options > Project and select Simulation on the left of the Project Options dialog.
2. Select the Use fUML Decision Semantics value check box so that the value becomes false. The value is false by default in the UML mode.

- A decision value can be passed as a parameter of Behavior of an opaque expression through a pin of Action. If a valid return value is from an assigned Behavior of a Guard, the body of the Guard will be ignored. However, if the Behavior is <unspecified>, the body of the Guard will be used.

Most of the elements on an Activity diagram are supported as follows

- Control Flow
- Object Flow (Transformation is not supported)
- Input Pin (Illegal type is not supported)
- Output Pin
- Activity Final Node
- Flow Final Node
- Activity Parameter Node: The parameter of the Activity Parameter Node must be defined. If the Activity Parameter Node violates the rule, Simulation will print a warning message in the **Console** pane .
- Decision Node
- Merge Node
- Join Node
- Fork Node
- Structured Activity Node
- Conditional Node
- Loop Node: the setup part will not be executed as the fUML specification. The number of Input Pins of the Loop Node must be equal to Output Pins. If the Loop Node violates the rule, Simulation will print an error message in the **Console** pane .
- Expansion Region
- Expansion Node
- Object Node
- Central Buffer Node
- Data Store Node
- Actions
- AcceptEventAction
- AddStructuralFeatureValueAction
- CallBehaviorAction
- CallOperationAction
- ClearAssociationAction
- ClearStructuralFeatureAction
- CreateLinkAction
- CreateObjectAction
- DestroyLinkAction
- DestroyObjectAction
- OpaqueAction
- ReadExtentAction
- ReadIsClassifiedObjectAction
- ReadLinkAction
- ReadSelfAction
- ReadStructuralFeatureAction
- ReclassifyObjectAction
- ReduceAction
- RemoveStructuralFeatureValueAction
- SendSignalAction
- StartObjectBehaviorAction
- TestIdentityAction
- ValueSpecificationAction

##### fUML engine settings

You can customize Behaviors of the fUML engine for Simulation to match your project Requirements through the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project options' space='']** dialog as shown below.

To customize the fUML engine settings in the **Project options** dialog

1. On the main menu, click Options and select Project . The Project options dialog opens.
2. On the left pane, click General > Simulation .
3. Go to the fUML Engine group and set any of the properties as desired. [ATTACHMENT filename='settings_for_fUML_engine.png']

The following table describes the project options of the fUML engine.

| Property | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Use fUML Decision semantics | If set to true (false by default when in the UML mode), all Guards will be solved to true when the flowing token value matches the Guard value (instead of evaluating every Guard as Boolean operation). |
| Auto Create fUML Object of Output | If set to true, automatically create an fUML object of output. |
| Pass Caller Context | If set to true, pass the caller context to the called Behavior if it does not have its own context specified. Otherwise, use the Behavior itself as context. |
| Terminate Nested Behaviors | If set to true, terminate nested Part Behaviors if their parent object Behavior is terminated. |
| Terminate Streaming Behaviors by Output Parameter Multiplicity | If set to true, a streaming Activity terminates when each of its output parameters receives a cumulative number of values equal to the upper bound of the parameter multiplicity. If set to false, a streaming Activity terminates only when forced by the Activity final node or termination of the Activity that invoked it. |
| Allow Concurrent Allocated Activities | If set to true, Activities can be executed in parallel even if the allocated resource is busy. If set to false, only one allocated Activity is executed for an object represented in an Activity Partition.If the Allow Concurrent Alllocated Activities option is set to true, it is recommended to use a time event to control the execution of parallel activities. A time event will increase the simulation performance in case one parallelly executed activity needs to get a response from another in order to start. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can instantiate a nested composite structure using the standard PSCS construction mechanism by default.

##### ReadLine support

ReadLine is a function that allows the user to enter value through the input line on the **Console** pane. A Call Behavior Action can be set Behavior as **ReadLine [fUML_Library::BasicInputOutput]** using **fUML_Library.mdzip** from the **Use Project** dialog. Before using the ReadLine function, you need to include **fUML_Library.mdzip** in the project first.

To open the **Use Project** dialog and include **fUML_Library.mdzip**

1. Click File > Use Project > Use Local Project from the main menu to open the Use Project dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. In the **Select a project to use** area, select the **From predefined location** option.

3. In the **Paths to used projects** list, select **<install.root>\modelLibraries**.

4. In the directory tree list, select **fUML-Library** and click **Next**> to proceed to the next step.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

5. You will be at the **Specify usage options** step. Click **Finish**. The **Question** dialog opens to ask you about showing auxiliary resources in the Containment tree. Click **Yes**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Then a Call Behavior Action can now be set Behavior as a ReadLine Element. The ReadLine Element will be shown with two default Pins, i.e., result and errorStatus. During the simulation, the ReadLine Element is executed to allow entering value through the input line on the **Console** pane. The result of the ReadLine Element can be used by other Elements with any proper data types, e.g., Guard, as in the following figure

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### ReadLine support allows entering value through the input line on the Console pane.

##### Guards in Swimlanes

Simulation supports Guards in Swimlanes in the Activity diagram as shown in the figure below. See also [Using Guards on Transitions](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Using+Guards+on+Transitions) and [Swimlane](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Swimlane).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Guards in Swimlanes are supported in the Activity diagram.

129494512

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Decision and Merge' space='MT']
- Behavior
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space='MT']

You will be at the <strong>Specify usage options</strong> step. Click <strong>Finish</strong>. The ReadLine Element will be shown with two default Pins, i.e., result and errorStatus. The result of the ReadLine Element can be used by other Elements with any proper data types, e.g., Guard, as in the following figure</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="7572 Readline Support.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">ReadLine support allows entering value through the input line on the Console pane.</h6><h3><br />Guards in Swimlanes</h3><p>Simulation supports Guards in Swimlanes in the Activity diagram as shown in the figure below. See also <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Using+Guards+on+Transitions">Using Guards on Transitions</a> and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Swimlane">Swimlane</a>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="9906-1.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Guards in Swimlanes are supported in the Activity diagram.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c69330f-e533-4a29-b90b-7e9e7f5f1bf3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">129494512</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Decision and Merge" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MMA/Behavior">Behavior</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MT" ri:content-title="Action" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570340 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00522: Adapting models for State Machine simulation

- page_id: `249570340`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570340/Adapting+models+for+State+Machine+simulation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:11.180+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > State Machine simulation
- labels: ['adapting-model-for-state-machine-simulation']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

116136583

116136585

116136584

Currently, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can simulate only those elements whose types are specified in the supported elements table in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Supported elements' space='']. Thus, you must modify your model so that only the supported (executable) elements are included in your State Machine diagram.

State Machine simulation can be adapted in several different ways, as outlined below:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining Triggers on Transitions' space='']Defining Triggers on Transitions Changing the States of runtime objects on Transitions by defining Triggers on Transitions.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Guards on Transitions' space=''] Specifying Guard conditions on Transitions by using any action languages.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State' space='']Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State Defining Behaviors that can be an Activity, a State Machine, an Interaction, or an OpaqueBehavior of States for Activities.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Internal Transition' space=''] 
Creating an Internal Transition to change the behavior without changing the overall state.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Signal properties mapping to Behavior parameters' space='']Signal properties mapping to Behavior parameters Mapping Signal properties to Behavior parameters by sending Signals to target objects to carry values to input parameters.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State activation semantics' space='']State activation semantics Specifying State Activation semantics regarding when Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit activates Entry Behaviors on activating entries of States.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Completion Events and Transitions' space='']Completion Events and Transitions Enabling or disabling completion of Events and Transitions during State Machine simulation.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Deferred Events' space='']Deferred Events Utilizing deferrable Triggers to save deferred Events for future processing until States are entered.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b10c121c-b74e-4610-adcd-30c44833ab8d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">116136583</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02408404-d1f0-4e44-8dfe-fc86a9f9453b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">116136585</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="006157c4-5082-4940-b8d4-39938c143ac7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">116136584</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Currently, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit can simulate only those elements whose types are specified in the supported elements table in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Supported elements" /></ac:link>. Thus, you must modify your model so that only the supported (executable) elements are included in your State Machine diagram. </p><p><br /></p><p>State Machine simulation can be adapted in several different ways, as outlined below:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining Triggers on Transitions" /><ac:link-body>Defining Triggers on Transitions<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Changing the States of runtime objects on Transitions by defining Triggers on Transitions.<br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Guards on Transitions" /></ac:link><br />Specifying Guard conditions on Transitions by using any action languages.<br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State" /><ac:link-body>Behaviors on Entry, Exit, and Do Activities of a State<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Defining Behaviors that can be an Activity, a State Machine, an Interaction, or an OpaqueBehavior of States for Activities.<br /><br /></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Internal Transition" /></ac:link><br />Creating an Internal Transition to change the behavior without changing the overall state.<br /></span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><br /></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Signal properties mapping to Behavior parameters" /><ac:link-body>Signal properties mapping to Behavior parameters<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Mapping Signal properties to Behavior parameters by sending Signals to target objects to carry values to input parameters.<br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State activation semantics" /><ac:link-body>State activation semantics<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Specifying State Activation semantics regarding when Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit activates Entry Behaviors on activating entries of States.<br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Completion Events and Transitions" /><ac:link-body>Completion Events and Transitions<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Enabling or disabling completion of Events and Transitions during State Machine simulation.<br /><br /></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Deferred Events" /><ac:link-body>Deferred Events<br /></ac:link-body></ac:link>Utilizing deferrable Triggers to save deferred Events for future processing until States are entered.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249571396 space=MMA version=1 -->
## PAGE 00523: Adding a reset Activity to the ready State

- page_id: `249571396`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249571396/Adding+a+reset+Activity+to+the+ready+State
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-10-09T04:52:45.782+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > Tutorial > Stopwatch model sample > Creating an executable stopwatch model > Adding Activities to the ready and running States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a **reset** Activity to the Entry to the **ready** State

1. Right-click the ready State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram and select Specification to open the State's Specification window.
2. Select the Entry row and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3-dot-btn.png'] button. A context menu will open.
3. Select Activity . A new Activity will be created for the entry to the ready State. [ATTACHMENT filename='specification-of-state-ready.png']
4. Name the created Activity "reset" .
5. Click Close to close the Specification window. The reset Activity will be added to the entry to the ready State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram as shown . [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav0f7253037c7049b9c8ab0aaa0450c45a.png']
6. Add an Activity diagram to the created reset Activity of the ready State by right-clicking the reset Activity in the containment browser and select New Diagram > Activity Diagram . A new Activity diagram will be created. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav52e8e6b88aa2892122cd86fc1e4225a3.png']
7. Use the default name of the diagram, which is "reset".
8. Add a CallBehaviorAction to the resetTime Activity on the reset Activity diagram by dragging the resetTime Activity from the containment browser to the reset Activity Diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddav359c7ae7ada3a14e838088b1255904ba.png']
9. Complete the reset Activity by adding an Initial stage and a Final Activity stage to the reset Activity diagram and connect the two stages and the resetTime Action using Control Flow , which is on the Activity Diagram toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddavfd0f163c0d3b56bd0fce375d9b07eccf.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a <strong>reset</strong> Activity to the Entry to the <strong>ready</strong> State</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the <strong>ready</strong> State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram and select <strong>Specification</strong> to open the State's Specification window.</li><li>Select the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Entry</strong> row and click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3-dot-btn.png" /></ac:image> button. A context menu will open.</li><li>Select <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Activity</strong>. A new Activity will be created for the entry to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">ready</strong> State. <br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Adding an Activity to the Entry of the Ready State" ac:alt="Adding an Activity to the Entry of the Ready State"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specification-of-state-ready.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Name the created Activity <em style="line-height: 1.42857;">&quot;reset&quot;</em>.</li><li>Click <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Close</strong> to close the Specification window. The reset Activity will be added to the entry to the ready State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram as shown .<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Adding the Reset Activity to the Entry of the Ready State" ac:alt="Adding the Reset Activity to the Entry of the Ready State"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav0f7253037c7049b9c8ab0aaa0450c45a.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Add an Activity diagram to the created <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">reset</strong> Activity of the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">ready</strong> State by right-clicking the reset Activity in the containment browser and select <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">New Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Activity </strong><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Diagram</strong>. A new Activity diagram will be created.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav52e8e6b88aa2892122cd86fc1e4225a3.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><br /></li><li>Use the default name of the diagram, which is &quot;reset&quot;.</li><li>Add a CallBehaviorAction to the resetTime Activity on the reset Activity diagram by dragging the resetTime Activity from the containment browser to the reset Activity Diagram.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Adding the ResetTimeAction to the ResetActivity of the ResetActivity Diagram" ac:alt="Adding the ResetTimeAction to the ResetActivity of the ResetActivity Diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav359c7ae7ada3a14e838088b1255904ba.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Complete the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">reset</strong> Activity by adding an Initial stage and a Final Activity stage to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">reset</strong> Activity diagram and connect the two stages and the resetTime Action using <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Control</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Flow</strong>, which is on the Activity Diagram toolbar. <br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="A Complete Model of the ResetActivity" ac:alt="A Complete Model of the ResetActivity"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavfd0f163c0d3b56bd0fce375d9b07eccf.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><p> </p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Next, we are going to add some Activities to the **ready** and **running** States. These Activities will be executed when these States are entered.

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249571404 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00525: Adding an increase activity to the running state

- page_id: `249571404`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249571404/Adding+an+increase+activity+to+the+running+state
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:24.248+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > Tutorial > Stopwatch model sample > Creating an executable stopwatch model > Adding Activities to the ready and running States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Next, we are going to create an increased Activity, which will be called when the running State is reached. To add the Activity to the running State, follow the same steps used to create the **reset** Activity for the entry to the **ready** State.

To add an Increase Activity to the entry to the running State

1. Right-click the running State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram and select Specification to open the State's Specification window.
2. Select the Entry row and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3-dot-btn.png'] button. A context menu will open. Select Activity to add a new Activity to the Entry to the running State.
3. Name the created Activity "increase" .
4. Click Close to close the Specification window. The increase Activity will be added to the entry to the running State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram as shown in the following figure [ATTACHMENT filename='worddave0a06c4dc481ca850e9ec15da65c084f.png']
5. Add an Activity diagram to the created increase Activity and use the default name, which is "increase".
6. Add a CallBehaviorAction to the increaseTime Activity on the increase Activity diagram by dragging the increaseTime Activity from the containment browser to the increase Activity diagram.
7. Add an Initial stage and a Final Activity stage to the increase Activity diagram and connect the two stages and the increaseTime action with Control Flow , which is on the Activity Diagram toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='worddavbceebb6243daff43453f3f6f09db8037.png']

At this point, the StopWatch has been completely modeled. You can now execute the StopWatch class using Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

To add the Activity to the running State, follow the same steps used to create the <strong>reset</strong> Activity for the entry to the <strong>ready</strong> State. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></p><p>To add an Increase Activity to the entry to the running State</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the <strong>running</strong> State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram and select <strong>Specification</strong> to open the State's Specification window.</li><li>Select the Entry row and click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3-dot-btn.png" /></ac:image> button. A context menu will open. Select <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Activity</strong> to add a new Activity to the Entry to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">running</strong> State.</li><li>Name the created Activity <em style="line-height: 1.42857;">&quot;increase&quot;</em>.</li><li>Click <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Close</strong> to close the Specification window. The increase Activity will be added to the entry to the running State on the StopWatch State Machine diagram as shown in the following figure<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Adding the Increase Activity to the Entry of the Running State" ac:alt="Adding the Increase Activity to the Entry of the Running State"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddave0a06c4dc481ca850e9ec15da65c084f.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Add an Activity diagram to the created <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increase</strong> Activity and use the default name, which is &quot;increase&quot;.</li><li>Add a CallBehaviorAction to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increaseTime</strong> Activity on the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increase</strong> Activity diagram by dragging the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increaseTime</strong> Activity from the containment browser to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increase</strong> Activity diagram.</li><li>Add an Initial stage and a Final Activity stage to the <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">increase</strong> Activity diagram and connect the two stages and the increaseTime action with <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Control</strong><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Flow</strong>, which is on the Activity Diagram toolbar. <br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="A Complete Model of the Increase Activity" ac:alt="A Complete Model of the Increase Activity"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavbceebb6243daff43453f3f6f09db8037.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />At this point, the StopWatch has been completely modeled. You can now execute the StopWatch class using Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit.</p>

## PAGE 00526: Adding breakpoints

- page_id: `249570316`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570316/Adding+breakpoints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-28T10:00:53.746+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation debugging > Breakpoints
- labels: ['adding-breakpoints']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add a Breakpoint to a model element using the model's **Add** **Breakpoint(s)** context menu.

To add a Breakpoint to a model element

- Right-click a model element either in the containment browser or on a symbol of the model element on the diagram and select Simulation > Add Breakpoint(s) .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249570862 space=MMA version=2 -->
## PAGE 00527: Adjunct property

- page_id: `249570862`
- space_key: `MMA`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MMA/pages/249570862/Adjunct+property
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-19T14:56:17.571+02:00`
- ancestors: Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation > User Guide > Simulation of SysML models > Supported SysML elements
- labels: ['adjunct-property']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Adjunct Property is a property to which the stereotype «AdjunctProperty» is applied. Its tag definition **principal** **: Element[1]** is for an element to determine the value of an adjunct property. With regard to the SysML Specification, a principal can be a Connector, a CallAction, an ObjectNode, a Variable, a Parameter, a Submachine State, or an InteractionUse. Now Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports the adjunct property whose principal is a CallBehaviorAction, a CallOperationAction, an ActivityParameterNode, or a Parameter.

If you use an adjunct property to represent an entry/do/exit behavior of a state, an effect behavior of a transition, and a method behavior of a call operation in a CallMessage in MagicDraw SysML plugin and Cameo System Modeler, you can use Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to show you the value of such adjunct property during simulation.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

**•** CallBehaviorAction and CallOperationAction are subtypes of CallAction. 
**•** ActivityParameterNode is a subtype of ObjectNode.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Executing an object with Adjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties.

The preceding shows an adjunct property in the **Integrator** activity. The adjunct property is typed by the activity **TimeDifferent**. Besides having the Integrator activity as a classifier behavior, the block **Integrator** also contains a classifier behavior property typed by the Integrator activity (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Classifier Behavior property' space=''] for more information about the classifier behavior property). When the Integrator object is initialized from the block **Integrator**, the behavior simulation of its classifier behavior will start and be set as a value of the classifier behavior property. While executing the classifier behavior, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will activate the call behavior action **getTimeDiff**, which is the principal of the adjunct property. It will create the behavior simulation of the **getTimeDiff** and set it as the value of the adjunct property in the **Variables** pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Adjunct Property is a property to which the stereotype «AdjunctProperty» is applied. Its tag definition <strong>principal</strong> <strong>: Element[1]</strong> is for an element to determine the value of an adjunct property. With regard to the SysML Specification, a principal can be a Connector, a CallAction, an ObjectNode, a Variable, a Parameter, a Submachine State, or an InteractionUse. Now Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit supports the adjunct property whose principal is a CallBehaviorAction, a CallOperationAction, an ActivityParameterNode, or a Parameter. </p><p>If you use an adjunct property to represent an entry/do/exit behavior of a state, an effect behavior of a transition, and a method behavior of a call operation in a CallMessage in MagicDraw SysML plugin and Cameo System Modeler, you can use Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit to show you the value of such adjunct property during simulation. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cee7410-4acb-4e4c-8765-ac3cb789e79f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"><strong>•</strong></span> CallBehaviorAction and CallOperationAction are subtypes of CallAction.<br /><span style="color: rgb(125,133,154);"><strong>•</strong></span> ActivityParameterNode is a subtype of ObjectNode. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Executing an Object withAdjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties" ac:alt="Executing an Object withAdjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties"><ri:attachment ri:filename="execute_objectwithAdjunct.png" /></ac:image><br />Executing an object with Adjunct and Classifier Behavior Properties.</h6><p>The preceding shows an adjunct property in the <strong>Integrator</strong> activity. The adjunct property is typed by the activity <strong>TimeDifferent</strong>. Besides having the Integrator activity as a classifier behavior, the block <strong>Integrator</strong> also contains a classifier behavior property typed by the Integrator activity (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Classifier Behavior property" /></ac:link> for more information about the classifier behavior property). When the Integrator object is initialized from the block <strong>Integrator</strong>, the behavior simulation of its classifier behavior will start and be set as a value of the classifier behavior property. While executing the classifier behavior, Magic Model Analyst / Cameo Simulation Toolkit will activate the call behavior action <strong>getTimeDiff</strong>, which is the principal of the adjunct property. It will create the behavior simulation of the <strong>getTimeDiff</strong> and set it as the value of the adjunct property in the <strong>Variables</strong> pane. </p><p> </p>
````
